createpolyphony.configuration.title: Create: Polyphony Configuration
Maximum simultaneously active synth voices before oldest notes are culled.
createpolyphony.configuration.ringBuffer: Size of the PCM bridge buffer between synth generation and Minecraft audio playback.
createpolyphony.configuration.pumpChunkB: Target PCM bytes per synth render chunk. Lower values improve responsiveness; higher values reduce CPU wakeups.
createpolyphony.configuration.scheduling: Look-ahead delay applied to incoming note events. Higher values improve stability under jitter; lower values reduce perceived input-to-audio delay.
createpolyphony.configuration.clockSyncI: How often the client re-syncs clock offset with the server. Lower values track drift more aggressively; higher values reduce sync traffic.
createpolyphony.configuration.adaptiveMi: Lower bound for adaptive stream subchunk size. Higher values are steadier but less responsive.
createpolyphony.configuration.adaptiveMa: Upper bound for adaptive stream subchunk size.
createpolyphony.configuration.adaptiveTa: Target render time budget per adaptive subchunk in nanoseconds.
createpolyphony.configuration.adaptiveEw: Controller smoothing factor (0..1): lower = steadier, higher = more reactive.
createpolyphony.configuration.synthSound: Chooses which Minecraft sound category controls instrument playback volume. Defaults to RECORDS.
createpolyphony.configuration.deployerPl: Controls deployer automation playback behavior: CONTINUOUS_POWERED keeps holders active between interactions for smooth playback, while INTERACTION_ONLY only plays around full deploy interactions.
createpolyphony.configuration.oneManBand: One Man Band Uses All GM Programs
When enabled, One Man Band plays raw GM programs (including synth lead, organ, etc). When disabled, it is limited to your explicitly supported instrument families.
createpolyphony.configuration.audibleDis: Maximum distance, in blocks, at which an instrument note can be heard. Decoupled from server simulation distance so playback range is not tied to chunk loading.
createpolyphony.configuration.selfPlayVo: Volume multiplier applied to instruments you are personally holding. Acts as a pre-attenuation PCM gain - values above 1.0 amplify, with hard-clip protection.
createpolyphony.configuration.otherPlayV: Volume multiplier applied to instruments held by other players, mobs, and deployers (positional). Useful for boosting external instrument loudness without changing the global Records slider.
createpolyphony.configuration.falloffMul: Multiplies the distance at which positional instrument audio fades out. 1.0 = audio reaches its maximum hearable distance; 0.5 = falls off twice as fast; 2.0 = carries twice as far. Higher values keep instruments loud closer to the source.
